seoTitle: Fix Cloudflare's Verify You Are Human Loop (2026)
metaDescription: Stuck in an endless Cloudflare Turnstile challenge? Clear cookies, sync your clock, disable extensions, and try incognito mode to break the loop.
intro: An endless Cloudflare 'Verify You Are Human' loop is usually caused by something local to your browser or network, not the website itself. This guide walks through the most common fixes, from clearing corrupted cookies to checking your system clock and switching networks.

steps.heading: Clear site cookies and cache
steps.body: Open your browser's privacy settings and clear cookies and cached data for the site that's stuck in the loop.

steps.heading: Sync your system clock
steps.body: Check your device's date and time settings and force a sync with network time servers, since clock drift can break the cryptographic handshake.

steps.heading: Disable conflicting extensions
steps.body: Open your extensions manager and temporarily turn off ad-blockers, custom user-agent switchers, or privacy filters that could be altering the challenge script.

steps.heading: Test in an incognito window
steps.body: Open a fresh incognito or private window to rule out corrupted local browser states.

steps.heading: Switch networks or drop your VPN
steps.body: Turn off crowded public VPN connections or cycle your router to clear a poor IP reputation, and note the Ray ID at the bottom of the page if you need to report the issue to the site admin.

faq.question: Why does Cloudflare keep failing to verify me even though I'm not a bot?
faq.answer: This is usually caused by a mismatch between your browser and expected signals, often from a bad IP reputation, corrupted cookies, or an extension interfering with the challenge script.

faq.question: Does using a VPN cause the verification loop?
faq.answer: Yes, public or shared VPN IPs often have poor reputations from prior abuse, which can trigger repeated Cloudflare challenges.

faq.question: What is the Ray ID and why does it matter?
faq.answer: The Ray ID is a unique identifier for your specific request, shown at the bottom of the challenge page, which the site's administrator can use to look up exactly what triggered the block.

faq.question: Will clearing my cookies log me out of other sites?
faq.answer: Clearing cookies for a specific site only affects that site's saved data; using your browser's site-specific privacy settings avoids logging you out everywhere else.
